title: Maria Tallchief Prima Ballerina
titleZh: 玛利亚·塔尔奇夫：首席芭蕾舞女演员

q.en: What was Maria Tallchief's birth name?
q.zh: 玛利亚·塔尔奇夫出生时的名字是什么？

options.zh: 玛利亚·帕申
伊丽莎白·玛丽·塔尔 Chief（小名贝蒂·玛丽）

options.en: Marjorie Nijinska according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 玛乔丽·尼金斯卡（按书中另一种说法）
露丝·奥色治

explain.en: The book says: 'Elizabeth Marie Tall Chief—nicknamed Betty Marie—was born on January 24, 1925.'
explain.zh: 书中说：“伊丽莎白·玛丽·塔尔 Chief——小名贝蒂·玛丽——出生于1925年1月24日。”

q.zh: 玛利亚·塔尔奇夫出生在哪里？

options.zh: 伊利诺伊州芝加哥

options.en: Fairfax, Oklahoma, on the Osage Reservation
options.zh: 俄克拉荷马州费尔法克斯的奥色治保留地
纽约市

options.en: Los Angeles, California according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 加利福尼亚州洛杉矶（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book states she 'was born on January 24, 1925, in Fairfax, Oklahoma. She was born on the Osage Reservation.'
explain.zh: 书中说她“1925年1月24日出生于俄克拉荷马州费尔法克斯，出生在奥色治保留地”。

q.en: In ballet, what is a 'prima ballerina'?
q.zh: 在芭蕾中，“prima ballerina（首席女舞者）”是什么？

options.en: The leading female dancer of a ballet company
options.zh: 芭蕾舞团中担任主演的女舞者

options.en: A kind of costume according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 一种服装（按书中另一种说法）
一个舞蹈动作
一种音乐

explain.en: The book says Tallchief became 'the New York City Ballet's first prima ballerina, or leading female dancer.'
explain.zh: 书中说塔尔奇夫成为“纽约市芭蕾舞团第一位prima ballerina，即首席女舞者”。

q.en: Which 1949 role made Maria Tallchief famous?
q.zh: 1949年的哪个角色让玛利亚·塔尔奇夫成名？

options.zh: 《火鸟》
《胡桃夹子》里的糖梅仙子
《百万美元美人鱼》
《天鹅湖》

explain.en: The book says: 'Everything changed when Tallchief danced in Firebird in 1949. The role made her famous.'
explain.zh: 书中说：“1949年塔尔奇夫出演《火鸟》后，一切都改变了。这个角色让她成名。”

q.en: According to the book, what made Tallchief's dancing special?
q.zh: 根据书中内容，塔尔奇夫的舞蹈特别之处在哪里？

options.en: She refused to perform in other countries according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 她拒绝去其他国家演出（按书中另一种说法）
她只跳慢舞

options.en: She was strong, graceful, light on her feet, and danced in a grand yet free way
options.zh: 她既有力又优雅，脚步轻快，舞蹈大气而自由
她完全照搬俄罗斯舞者的跳法

explain.en: The book says people saw she was 'a strong and graceful dancer,' 'light on her feet and exact,' and danced 'in a grand yet free way that set her apart.'
explain.zh: 书中说人们看到她“强壮又优雅”，脚步“轻快而精准”，并以“大气而自由、与众不同的方式”舞蹈。

q.en: Why is Maria Tallchief an important figure in ballet?
q.zh: 为什么玛利亚·塔尔奇夫是芭蕾史上重要的人物？

options.en: She was America's first prima ballerina and the first Native American ballet star
options.zh: 她是美国第一位首席女舞者，也是第一位原住民芭蕾明星
她写了这本书的词汇表

options.en: She was the first woman to fly a plane according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 她是第一位驾驶飞机的女子（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）
她发明了钢琴

explain.en: The Legacy section says she was 'the first prima ballerina from the United States' and 'ballet's first Native American star.'
explain.zh: “传承”一节说她是“美国第一位首席女舞者”，也是“芭蕾界第一位原住民明星”。

q.en: Who was George Balanchine, and how was he connected to Tallchief?
q.zh: 乔治·巴兰钦是谁？他和塔尔奇夫有什么关联？

options.zh: 她的钢琴老师

options.en: The president who gave her an award according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 给她颁奖的总统（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）
她的姐姐

options.en: A famous choreographer who created a new American ballet style and was her muse and husband
options.zh: 一位著名的编舞家，创造了美国芭蕾新风格，是她的灵感来源也是丈夫

explain.en: Balanchine was 'one of the twentieth century's most important choreographers'; he made her his muse and they married in 1946.
explain.zh: 巴兰钦是“20世纪最重要的编舞家之一”；他让塔尔奇夫成为自己的灵感女神，两人于1946年结婚。

options.zh: 一种奖项
一种乐器

options.en: A Native American tribe according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 一个原住民部落（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: A person who chooses the style and arrangement of steps for a dance performance
options.zh: 为舞蹈表演设计动作风格与编排的人

explain.en: The glossary defines 'choreographer' as 'a person who chooses the style and arrangement of steps and moves for a dance performance.'
explain.zh: 词汇表将“choreographer”定义为“为舞蹈表演设计动作风格与编排的人”。

q.en: After retiring from performing in 1965, what did Tallchief do in Chicago?
q.zh: 1965年退出舞台表演后，塔尔奇夫在芝加哥做了什么？

options.zh: 她搬回了俄克拉荷马

options.en: She founded the Chicago City Ballet in 1980
options.zh: 她于1980年创办了芝加哥市立芭蕾舞团
她成了电影明星

options.en: She stopped all dance work according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 她彻底不再从事舞蹈工作（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: After moving to Chicago, 'She started the Chicago City Ballet in 1980' and was its creative director until 1987.
explain.zh: 搬到芝加哥后，“她于1980年创办了芝加哥市立芭蕾舞团”，并担任艺术总监直到1987年。

q.en: What does the book say about Tallchief's lasting legacy?
q.zh: 书中是如何描述塔尔奇夫持久影响的？

options.zh: 她让卡车流行起来

options.en: She ended the Osage Reservation according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 她取消了奥色治保留地（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: She helped inspire a new style of ballet and opened its doors to people of all backgrounds
options.zh: 她帮助催生了芭蕾的新风格，并为其向各背景的人敞开大门
她写了儿童书

explain.en: The Legacy section says she 'helped inspire a new style of ballet' and 'opened its doors to people of all backgrounds.'
explain.zh: “传承”一节说她“帮助催生了芭蕾的新风格”，并“为其向各背景的人敞开大门”。

prompt.en: Maria Tallchief was the first Native American ballet star. Who is someone who opened doors for others in your life, and how?
prompt.zh: 玛利亚·塔尔奇夫是第一位原住民芭蕾明星。在你的生活中，有谁为别人敞开了大门？是怎样做到的？

en: Think about the book's idea that she 'opened its doors to people of all backgrounds.'
Use details about her hard work and achievements to shape your answer.
zh: 想想书中“她为各背景的人敞开了大门”这一说法。
运用书中关于她努力与成就的细节来组织你的回答。
